projects.title: Pushfolio
projects.summary: An AI agent pipeline that reads GitHub commit history and auto-publishes structured project summaries to a personal portfolio page.
projects.problem_solved: Commit history contains real problem-solving evidence but no tool surfaces it in a readable narrative form.
projects.how_i_solved_it: Built a three-subagent pipeline — fetcher, analyzer, publisher — where hotspot files (highest commit count) drive the 'challenge' narrative rather than README alone.

projects.title: DSA & Algorithm Lab
projects.summary: A personal coding scratchpad for practicing data structures and algorithms — AVL trees, stacks, client-server patterns, and encryption — written in Python while learning the concepts.
projects.problem_solved: Textbook pseudocode skips the edge cases and language-specific details that only surface when you actually type the code and run it.
projects.how_i_solved_it: Implemented each data structure as a standalone Python file, testing manually as I went — this repo is the record of trial-and-error, not a polished library.

projects.title: Vibe Code Tours
projects.summary: A bilingual (EN + Burmese) static site for a Myanmar AI coding bootcamp — Astro 5, Tailwind CSS, zero client JS by default. I contributed as part of a larger team, working within an established codebase and reviewing PRs from a growing cohort of student builders.
projects.problem_solved: Joining an active project mid-flight means inheriting someone else's design decisions, i18n system, and contribution flow — pushing code without breaking the Burmese locale pipeline or existing UI patterns.
projects.how_i_solved_it: Studied the existing Astro component conventions before touching anything, kept PRs small and scoped (self-hosted fonts, GPU-friendly animations, builder onboarding docs), and followed the project's CODEOWNERS/review flow to stay aligned with the lead.
